purpose: Assemble a deterministic downloadable bundle from declared files and manifest metadata for end-user delivery or archival export.

endpoints.description: Create a bundle from declared files and return its manifest plus output path.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Deterministic artifact bundle builder for downloads and exports.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use when a workflow needs to package selected files into one user-facing or archive-ready bundle.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use for arbitrary filesystem backup, encrypted secret transport, or remote CDN publication.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Select scoped files and a bundle name. 2) Call /bundles/build. 3) Serve or store the resulting bundle path.

failure_modes.condition: Requested file is missing or outside the declared root
failure_modes.symptom: 400 error identifying the invalid file path
failure_modes.recovery: Filter the file list to existing, in-scope artifacts

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Resolve all requested files against root_path, reject out-of-scope entries, optionally include a machine-readable manifest file, and generate a deterministic zip archive with stable file ordering.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Bundles become nondeterministic if file order or timestamps vary. Missing files should fail early rather than silently producing partial downloads.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Build from an explicit file list and add files in sorted order to the archive.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not recursively zip an entire root blindly and do not follow symlinks outside the root.
